div.h1: Van Emde Boas Layout: Subtree Memory Contiguity

p.strong: Nodes close in tree distance → stored close in memory distance

div.p: Legend: 🟢 Top subtree (contiguous) | 🔵 Bottom subtrees (each contiguous) | 🟠 Highlighted access path

ul.li: One cache miss loads entire subtree
Subsequent accesses are L1 hits (~4 cycles) Pointer-Based BST:

li.strong: Each access is a cache miss (~200 cycles)

g.text: Pointer BST| ~20 | One miss per level
Total: vEB is 4× faster than pointer-based

div.p: Contiguous Memory Region ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐ │ [R] [1] [2] │ [3] [7] [8] │ [4] [9] [A] │ [5] [B] [C] │ [6] [D] [E] │ │ Top Subtree│ Subtree 3 │ Subtree 4 │ Subtree 5 │ Subtree 6 │ └─────────────────────────────────────────┘ ▲ ▲ │ │ 3 nodes 3 nodes (12 bytes) (12 bytes) contiguous contiguous

li.strong: Entire subtree (3 nodes) fits in 1 cache line!
